commits.title: adds support for custom parsers for commit groups
commits.body:
Adds the ability for users to modify a set of default parsers and define
a set of custom parsers. Each parser consists of a pattern, a title, and
a skip field. When skip is true, any commits matching the pattern will
be omitted from changelog and version calculation. Otherwise the commit
will be considered for version calculation and will appear in the
changelog grouped under the parser title. To order the groups, users
should place a tag at the start of the title with the following form
"<!-- <two_digit_index> --><title>". Users can then sort groups in the
tera template via "sort(attribute="group")" and strip the leading tag
when displaying via "{{ group | striptags | trim }}".
commits.breaking_description:
The following changelog options have been deprecated
in toml config in support of the new default_parsers and
custom_parsers fields.
- skip_ci
- skip_chore
- skip_doc
- skip_test
- skip_style
- skip_refactor
- skip_perf
- skip_revert
- skip_miscellaneous
commits.raw_title: feat!: adds support for custom parsers for commit groups
commits.raw_message:
feat!: adds support for custom parsers for commit groups

Adds the ability for users to modify a set of default parsers and define
a set of custom parsers. Each parser consists of a pattern, a title, and
a skip field. When skip is true, any commits matching the pattern will
be omitted from changelog and version calculation. Otherwise the commit
will be considered for version calculation and will appear in the
changelog grouped under the parser title. To order the groups, users
should place a tag at the start of the title with the following form
"<!-- <two_digit_index> --><title>". Users can then sort groups in the
tera template via "sort(attribute="group")" and strip the leading tag
when displaying via "{{ group | striptags | trim }}".

BREAKING CHANGE: The following changelog options have been deprecated
in toml config in support of the new default_parsers and
custom_parsers fields.
- skip_ci
- skip_chore
- skip_doc
- skip_test
- skip_style
- skip_refactor
- skip_perf
- skip_revert
- skip_miscellaneous

[Issue #341](https://github.com/robgonnella/releasaurus/issues/341)

release.notes:
# [0.21.0-rc.3](https://github.com/robgonnella/releasaurus/compare/v0.21.0-rc.2...v0.21.0-rc.3) - 2026-06-25

### ❌ Breaking

[**breaking**]: adds support for custom parsers for commit groups [_(e737f87)_]() (Rob Gonnella)
> Adds the ability for users to modify a set of default parsers and define
a set of custom parsers. Each parser consists of a pattern, a title, and
a skip field. When skip is true, any commits matching the pattern will
be omitted from changelog and version calculation. Otherwise the commit
will be considered for version calculation and will appear in the
changelog grouped under the parser title. To order the groups, users
should place a tag at the start of the title with the following form
"<!-- <two_digit_index> --><title>". Users can then sort groups in the
tera template via "sort(attribute="group")" and strip the leading tag
when displaying via "{{ group | striptags | trim }}".
> The following changelog options have been deprecated
in toml config in support of the new default_parsers and
custom_parsers fields.
- skip_ci
- skip_chore
- skip_doc
- skip_test
- skip_style
- skip_refactor
- skip_perf
- skip_revert
- skip_miscellaneous

### 🐛 Bug Fixes
